name: Mattia Niccoli
heroTag: Geospatial Analysis · Earth Observation · GIS ·  Remote Sensing
heroSub: M.Sc. in Geography. Research Fellow at the Laboratory of Forest Geomatics, University of Florence. I work at the intersection of GIS, remote sensing, and data analysis — transforming heterogeneous geospatial data into knowledge for environmental management, urban planning, and beyond.
aboutTexts: I'm Mattia Niccoli, a geographer and spatial analyst. My work sits at the intersection of Earth Observation, GIS, and quantitative geography — I apply these methods to extract strategic insights from spatial data across a range of domains: environmental management, urban climate, forest monitoring, and every domain where geographic thinking represents an added value.

I am currently a Research Fellow at GeoLAB, the Laboratory of Forest Geomatics at the University of Florence, where I work with heterogeneous geospatial data — satellite imagery, geospatial dataset, LiDAR, georeferenced field data — across the full project cycle: data management, spatial and statistical analysis, cartographic production, and contributions to scientific publications.

I hold a Master's degree in Geography (110/110 cum Laude) from the University of Florence, and a Bachelor's in Economics (110/110 cum Laude). I work primarily in R and Python for spatial analysis, and with ArcGIS Pro, QGIS, and Google Earth Engine for remote sensing and cartography.
contactDesc: Interested in collaboration, geospatial projects, or just want to discuss geography and data? I'm always happy to connect.
footerText: © 2025 Mattia Niccoli — Built with GitHub Pages

projects.title: Voting Patterns in Florence & Sesto Fiorentino
projects.desc: Interactive maps at sezione elettorale level showing vote shares, percentage shifts, and turnout across Comunali 2024 and Regionali 2025. Built with R, Leaflet.js, and GeoJSON on GitHub Pages.

projects.title: Mapping Local Climate Zones in the Metropolitan Area of Florence
projects.desc: Integrated multitemporal Sentinel-2 imagery with ancillary geospatial layers to produce a Local Climate Zone map of Florence using a Random Forest classifier. Published in Bollettino della Società Geografica Italiana.
